verdict_reasoning: EREG is a single-pass transmembrane protein with a documented stable cell surface pool. The transmembrane pro-EREG precursor (169 aa) reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM domain and is targeted to basolateral and apical surfaces by specific trafficking motifs. Although it undergoes ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding to release soluble mature EREG, the membrane-anchored precursor is the canonical surface form with its own membrane integration and polarity-dependent trafficking. This qualifies as a classical surface receptor-like protein with documented cell surface residency.
